nlc: Verify that all City procurements are made by one of the methods authorized by these Rules, unless otherwise authorized by law.

rule: Except as otherwise provided in these Rules, contracts shall be awarded by competitive sealed bidding
nlc: Verify that contracts are awarded by competitive sealed bidding, except as otherwise provided in these Rules.
sql: SELECT * FROM contract_award WHERE SelectionMethodDescription <> 'Competitive Sealed Bids' AND (SpecialCaseReasonDescription IS NULL)

nlc: Verify that, in the case of construction, competitive sealed bidding from prequalified vendors or any other appropriate procurement method is used where deemed appropriate by the ACCO and in accordance with these Rules.

rule: A “Special Case” determination is not required for such procurements
nlc: Verify that a Special Case determination is not made for such procurements.
dc: ["SpecialCaseReasonDescription IS NULL", "CategoryDescription LIKE '%data processing equipment%'"], ["SpecialCaseReasonDescription IS NULL", "CategoryDescription LIKE '%similar services%'"]
sql: SELECT * FROM contract_award WHERE SpecialCaseReasonDescription IS NULL AND (CategoryDescription LIKE '%data processing equipment%' OR CategoryDescription LIKE '%similar services%')

rule: Agencies may elect to use one of the methods of source selection listed herein, after making the determination that it is not practicable or not advantageous to the City to use competitive sealed bidding as required by this section
nlc: Verify that agencies have made the determination that it is not practicable or not advantageous to the City to use competitive sealed bidding before electing to use one of the methods of source selection listed herein.

rule: A Special Case determination is required for each case in which one of the following methods of source selection is used: (i) Competitive sealed bidding from prequalified vendors, except as provided in Section 3-10(a) of these Rules;(ii) Competitive sealed bidding where the award will be made to the bidder whose bid represents the best value to the City;(iii) Competitive sealed proposals (including multi-step process);(iv) Competitive sealed proposals from prequalified vendors;(v) Negotiated acquisition;(vi) Sole source procurement;(vii) Demonstration project for innovative products, approaches, or technologies;(viii) Innovative procurement method; or (ix) Government-to-government purchase.
nlc: Verify that a Special Case determination is made for each case where the method of source selection used is one of the following: competitive sealed bidding from prequalified vendors (except as provided in Section 3-10(a) of these Rules), competitive sealed bidding where the award is made to the bidder whose bid represents the best value to the City, competitive sealed proposals (including multi-step process), competitive sealed proposals from prequalified vendors, negotiated acquisition, sole source procurement, demonstration project for innovative products, approaches, or technologies, innovative procurement method, or government-to-government purchase.
dc: ["SelectionMethodDescription in ('Competitive Sealed Bids','Competitive Sealed Bids/Pre-Qualified List','Competitive Sealed Proposals','Competitive Sealed Proposals/Pre-Qualified List','Negotiated Acquisition','Sole Source','Demonstration Project','Innovative Procurement','Government to Government')", "SpecialCaseReasonDescription IS NULL"]
sql: SELECT * FROM contract_award WHERE SelectionMethodDescription in ('Competitive Sealed Bids','Competitive Sealed Bids/Pre-Qualified List','Competitive Sealed Proposals','Competitive Sealed Proposals/Pre-Qualified List','Negotiated Acquisition','Sole Source','Demonstration Project','Innovative Procurement','Government to Government') AND SpecialCaseReasonDescription IS NULL

rule: A special case is a circumstance recognized by these Rules in which it is not practicable or not advantageous to the City to use competitive sealed bidding for one of the following reasons: (i) specifications cannot be made sufficiently definite and certain to permit selection based on bid price or evaluated bid price alone; (ii) judgment is required in evaluating competing proposals, and it is in the best interest of the City to require a balancing of price, quality, and other factors; (iii) it is in the best interest of the City for goods or standard services to be awarded on the basis of best value to the City by optimizing quality, cost, and efficiency; (iv) there is only one available source, as set forth in these Rules; (v) testing, experimentation, or evaluation is required to determine the feasibility and application of an innovative product, approach, or technology not currently used by the City; (vi) the need for advance screening of qualifications is paramount and prequalification is appropriate for one of the reasons set forth in these Rules; (vii) circumstances justifying the use of negotiated acquisition as set forth in these Rules; or (viii) to test and evaluate the feasibility and application of innovative procurement methods not currently used by the City or provided for in these Rules
nlc: Verify that a special case is recognized when it is not practicable or advantageous to use competitive sealed bidding for the following reasons: specifications cannot be made definite and certain for selection based on bid price alone; judgment is required for evaluating proposals with a balance of price, quality, and other factors; awarding goods or services based on best value by optimizing quality, cost, and efficiency is in the City's best interest; only one source is available; testing, experimentation, or evaluation is needed for innovative products or technologies not used by the City; advance screening of qualifications is paramount and prequalification is appropriate; circumstances justify negotiated acquisition; or to test and evaluate innovative procurement methods not used by the City.

rule: Upon determining that there is a special case, the Contracting Officer shall select the most competitive alternate method of source selection among those listed in Section 3-01 above which is practicable and advantageous to the City
nlc: Verify that when a special case is determined, the Contracting Officer selects the most competitive alternate method of source selection from those listed in Section 3-01 that is practicable and advantageous to the City.

rule: The determination that there is a special case, and the reasons that the method of source selection is the most competitive that is appropriate under the circumstances, shall be made in writing in advance of issuing solicitations, as part of any presolicitation review required by these Rules, and shall be approved by the ACCO
nlc: Verify that any determination of a special case, along with the reasons for the method of source selection being the most competitive under the circumstances, is documented in writing before issuing solicitations, as part of any required presolicitation review, and is approved by the ACCO.

rule: Procurements by negotiated acquisition shall require the written approval of the CCPO prior to initiating negotiations
nlc: Verify that all procurements by negotiated acquisition have the written approval of the CCPO before negotiations are initiated.
dc: ["SelectionMethodDescription = 'Negotiated Acquisition'", "AdditionalDescription1 not LIKE '%CCPO%'"], ["SelectionMethodDescription = 'Negotiated Acquisition'", "upper(OtherInfo1) not LIKE '%CCPO%'"]
sql: SELECT * FROM contract_award WHERE SelectionMethodDescription = 'Negotiated Acquisition' AND (AdditionalDescription1 not LIKE '%CCPO%' or OtherInfo1 not LIKE '%CCPO%')

rule: This section shall apply to all procurements made by competitive sealed bidding
nlc: Verify that this section applies to all procurements made by competitive sealed bidding.

rule: The IFB is used to initiate a competitive sealed bid procurement
nlc: Verify that the IFB is used to initiate a competitive sealed bid procurement.

rule: The Invitation for Bids shall include the following: (i) instructions and information to bidders concerning the bid submission requirements, including the time and date set for receipt of the bids; requirements for the electronic submission of bids, if any; time, date, and location of any pre-bid conferences (and a statement whether such conferences are mandatory); and the location where bids are to be submitted; (ii) the purchase description, delivery and performance schedule, and any special instructions necessary; (iii) the contract terms and conditions, including warranty and bonding or other security requirements, as applicable
nlc: Verify that the Invitation for Bids includes: (i) instructions and information to bidders concerning the bid submission requirements, including the time and date set for receipt of the bids, requirements for the electronic submission of bids if any, time, date, and location of any pre-bid conferences with a statement on whether such conferences are mandatory, and the location where bids are to be submitted; (ii) the purchase description, delivery and performance schedule, and any special instructions necessary; (iii) the contract terms and conditions, including warranty and bonding or other security requirements, as applicable.

rule: for construction, a statement that award shall be made to the lowest responsive and responsible bidder
nlc: Verify that for construction, the award is made to the lowest responsive and responsible bidder.

rule: for purchase of goods and standard services, a statement that award shall be made to the lowest responsive and responsible bidder or to the bidder whose bid represents the best value to the City by optimizing quality, cost and efficiency
nlc: Verify that for the purchase of goods and standard services, the award is made to the lowest responsive and responsible bidder or to the bidder whose bid represents the best value to the City by optimizing quality, cost, and efficiency.

rule: if not included in the bid documents, a notice of where vendors may obtain a copy of all contractual terms and conditions or other project-related material
nlc: Verify that if a notice is not included in the bid documents, there is a notice of where vendors may obtain a copy of all contractual terms and conditions or other project-related material.

rule: a provision that bidders should give specific attention to the identification of those portions of their bids that they deem to be confidential proprietary information or trade secrets and provide any justification why such materials, upon request, should not be disclosed by the City
nlc: Verify that bidders have identified portions of their bids as confidential proprietary information or trade secrets and have provided justification for why such materials should not be disclosed by the City upon request.

rule: bids may be withdrawn only after the expiration of fortyfive days from bid opening and only in writing received by the agency and in advance of award
nlc: Verify that bids are withdrawn only after the expiration of forty-five days from bid opening, only in writing received by the agency, and only in advance of award.

rule: contract award is subject to provisions of the MacBride Principles Law
nlc: Verify that the contract award complies with the provisions of the MacBride Principles Law.

rule: contract award is subject, if applicable, to Section 6-129 of the New York City Administrative Code (M/WBE and EBE program), as well as to applicable provisions of federal, State, and local laws and executive orders requiring affirmative action and equal employment opportunity
nlc: Verify that the contract award complies with Section 6-129 of the New York City Administrative Code (M/WBE and EBE program) if applicable, and adheres to the applicable provisions of federal, State, and local laws and executive orders requiring affirmative action and equal employment opportunity.

rule: where applicable, a notice that contract award is subject to completion of a VENDEX questionnaire and review of that information by the Department of Investigation
nlc: Verify that, where applicable, a notice is present indicating that the contract award is subject to the completion of a VENDEX questionnaire and the review of that information by the Department of Investigation.

rule: the name, address, and telephone number of a contact person to whom questions and correspondence relating to the bid solicitation can be addressed
nlc: Verify that the name, address, and telephone number of a contact person to whom questions and correspondence relating to the bid solicitation can be addressed are present.

rule: The New York City Comptroller is charged with the audit of contracts in New York City
nlc: Verify that the New York City Comptroller audits all contracts in New York City.

rule: Any vendor who believes that there has been unfairness, favoritism, or impropriety in the bid process should inform the Comptroller, Bureau of Contract Administration, 1 Centre Street, Room 727, New York, NY 10007, (212) 669- 2323
nlc: Verify that any vendor who believes there has been unfairness, favoritism, or impropriety in the bid process has informed the Comptroller, Bureau of Contract Administration, at 1 Centre Street, Room 727, New York, NY 10007, (212) 669-2323.

rule: the bidder will be required to comply, if applicable, with § 220/230 of the New York State Labor Law, § 6-109 of the New York Administrative Code, Mayoral Executive Order 102 of 2006, and with all other federal, State, and local labor laws and regulations, including but not limited to providing on-the-job training opportunities and payment of prevailing wages and living wages
nlc: Verify that the bidder complies, if applicable, with § 220/230 of the New York State Labor Law, § 6-109 of the New York Administrative Code, Mayoral Executive Order 102 of 2006, and all other federal, State, and local labor laws and regulations, including providing on-the-job training opportunities and payment of prevailing wages and living wages.

rule: where applicable for construction contracts, the following shall be additionally included: (A) a specific description and exact location of the construction and the date and time for the bidder to visit the job site when the solicitation requires a mandatory pre-bid visit to and examination of the job site; (B) a reference to all applicable documents which will subsequently become a part of the contract, such as the specifications and the terms and conditions approved by Corporation Counsel; (C) a statement establishing minimum insurance requirements which the City will require of the bidder if successful and conveying to the bidder any insurance coverage which the City will carry that will afford the contractor insurance coverage; (D) a statement that the bidder will be required to meet all licensing or permit requirements required to perform the construction; (E) a statement of instructions relative to the return of bid documents if the bidder elects not to submit a bid thereunder and notification to the City of the bidder declining to bid; (F) a statement that all bid documents must be returned to the City upon request; (G) the form in which the bid is to be submitted (either specified in the invitation for bids or referenced as part of the specifications or attached forms); (H) a requirement that for projects on which more than one prime contractor will be involved, all bidders examine the invitation for bid packages for all other parts of the project; and (I) a notice, for those contracts not otherwise subject to Section 6- 129 of the New York Administrative Code (M/WBE and EBE Program), that contract award is subject to the provisions of Section 6-108.1 of the New York City Administrative Code relating to the LBE program and its implementing rules; and (J) a requirement that, where the preparation of separate specifications is not required for plumbing and gas fitting; steam heating, hot water heating, ventilating and air conditioning apparatus; and electric wiring and standard illumination fixtures pursuant to New York General Municipal Law Section 101, the bidder shall submit with its bid a separate sealed list that names each subcontractor that the bidder will use to perform such work on the contract, and the agreed-upon amount to be paid to each
nlc: Verify that for applicable construction contracts, the following are included: (A) a specific description and exact location of the construction, and the date and time for the bidder to visit the job site when a mandatory pre-bid visit is required; (B) references to all applicable documents that will become part of the contract, such as specifications and terms and conditions approved by Corporation Counsel; (C) a statement of minimum insurance requirements for the bidder and any insurance coverage provided by the City; (D) a statement that the bidder must meet all licensing or permit requirements for the construction; (E) instructions for the return of bid documents if the bidder does not submit a bid, and notification to the City of the bidder's decision not to bid; (F) a statement that all bid documents must be returned to the City upon request; (G) the form in which the bid is to be submitted, as specified or referenced in the invitation for bids; (H) a requirement for all bidders to examine bid packages for all parts of the project when multiple prime contractors are involved; (I) a notice that contract award is subject to Section 6-108.1 of the New York City Administrative Code for contracts not subject to Section 6-129; and (J) a requirement for a separate sealed list of subcontractors and payment amounts for work not requiring separate specifications under New York General Municipal Law Section 101.

rule: If award will be made on the basis of best value to the City, such statement shall include how best value will be determined in accordance with Section 3-02(o) of these Rules
nlc: Verify that any award made on the basis of best value to the City includes a statement detailing how best value is determined in accordance with Section 3-02(o) of these Rules.

rule: The bidding time shall be not less than fifteen days before the bid opening date
nlc: Verify that the bidding time is at least fifteen days before the bid opening date.

rule: The IFB will provide a form on which the bidder must insert the bid price, or other information requested, if any, pursuant to Section 3-02(o)(1) of these Rules, and must sign and submit along with all other necessary submissions
nlc: Verify that the bidder has inserted the bid price or other requested information on the provided form, signed it, and submitted it along with all other necessary submissions.

rule: Bids must be typewritten or written legibly in ink
nlc: Verify that all bids are either typewritten or written legibly in ink.

rule: Erasures or alterations shall be initialed by the signer in ink or by electronic signature
nlc: Verify that all erasures or alterations are initialed by the signer either in ink or by electronic signature.

rule: All bids must be signed in ink or by electronic signature
nlc: Verify that all bids are signed in ink or by electronic signature.

rule: The bid invitation also will also require that the bid be submitted in a sealed envelope, addressed as required in the bid documents
nlc: Verify that each bid is submitted in a sealed envelope and is addressed as required in the bid documents.

rule: Where award will be made to the bidder whose bid represents the best value to the City, the IFB may also provide that other information requested, if any, may be submitted up to thirty (30) days from the bid opening by all bidders whose bids are to be considered pursuant to Section 3-02(o)(1)(iii) of these Rules.
nlc: Verify that all bidders whose bids are to be considered pursuant to Section 3-02(o)(1)(iii) have submitted any requested additional information within thirty (30) days from the bid opening.

rule: The IFB shall state that the submission of bid samples and descriptive literature, regardless of any attempt by a bidder to condition the bid, will not be deemed to vary any of the provisions of the IFB
nlc: Verify that the IFB states that the submission of bid samples and descriptive literature, regardless of any attempt by a bidder to condition the bid, is not deemed to vary any of the provisions of the IFB.

rule: IFBs or notices of their availability must be electronically mailed to a sufficient number of vendors, including all vendors on the appropriate citywide bidders list established by the CCPO for the purpose of securing competition
nlc: Verify that IFBs or notices of their availability are electronically mailed to a sufficient number of vendors, including all vendors on the appropriate citywide bidders list established by the CCPO for the purpose of securing competition.

rule: IFBs or notices of their availability may be sent electronically to vendors on agency-specific bidders lists, in addition to the appropriate citywide bidders list maintained by the CCPO, only with approval of the CCPO.
nlc: Verify that IFBs or notices of their availability are sent electronically to vendors on agency-specific bidders lists, in addition to the citywide bidders list maintained by the CCPO, only with the approval of the CCPO.

rule: Such IFBs or notices must be sent electronically at least fifteen (15) days in advance of the due date for bids, or at least twenty-two (22) days in advance of the due date for bids which are subject to Section 6-129 of the New York City Administrative Code (M/WBE and EBE program)
nlc: Verify that all IFBs or notices are sent electronically at least fifteen (15) days before the due date for bids, or at least twenty-two (22) days before the due date for bids subject to Section 6-129 of the New York City Administrative Code (M/WBE and EBE program).

rule: An agency may, upon request of a vendor, provide IFBs or notices by mail, fax, hand delivery, or otherwise
nlc: Verify that, upon request of a vendor, an agency provides IFBs or notices by mail, fax, hand delivery, or other means.

rule: Where the notice does not include all IFB documents, an additional five (5) days must be allowed
nlc: Verify that an additional five (5) days are allowed when the notice does not include all IFB documents.

rule: Notices of availability must indicate, at minimum: (A) the name of the agency and, if appropriate, the specific division or bureau soliciting the bids; (B) title and brief description of the goods, services, or construction required; (C) specific information about how, when, and where the IFB is available; (D) the required fee or deposit amount, if any, for obtaining the IFB; (E) the time, date, and location of any pre-bid conference or site visit, if any, and if attendance is mandatory; (F) the date, time, and location and, if applicable, a link to access the livestreaming of the opening, reading, and identification of bids on a publicly accessible website, for the receipt and opening of bids; (G) the name and phone number of the agency contact person, including email address, if applicable; and (H) the citywide bidders list used
nlc: Verify that notices of availability include at minimum: the name of the agency and, if appropriate, the specific division or bureau soliciting the bids; the title and brief description of the goods, services, or construction required; specific information about how, when, and where the IFB is available; the required fee or deposit amount, if any, for obtaining the IFB; the time, date, and location of any pre-bid conference or site visit, if any, and if attendance is mandatory; the date, time, and location and, if applicable, a link to access the livestreaming of the opening, reading, and identification of bids on a publicly accessible website, for the receipt and opening of bids; the name and phone number of the agency contact person, including email address, if applicable; and the citywide bidders list used.
dc: ["AgencyName IS NULL"], ["SectionName IS NULL"], ["ShortTitle IS NULL"], ["AdditionalDescription1 IS NULL"], ["AddressToRequest IS NULL"], ["ContractAmount IS NULL"], ["EventDate IS  NULL"], ["EventStreetAddress1 IS NULL"], ["DocumentLinks IS NULL"], ["ContactName IS NULL"], ["ContactPhone IS  NULL"], ["Email IS NULL"]
sql: SELECT * FROM contract_award WHERE AgencyName IS NULL OR SectionName IS NULL OR ShortTitle IS NULL OR AdditionalDescription1 IS NULL OR AddressToRequest IS NULL OR ContractAmount IS NULL OR EventDate IS  NULL OR EventStreetAddress1 IS NULL OR DocumentLinks IS NULL OR ContactName IS NULL OR ContactPhone IS  NULL OR Email IS NULL

rule: Notice of solicitation shall be published at lease once in the City Record and shall be posted on the City’s website in a location that is accessible by the public simultaneously with its publication not less than fifteen days before the bid opening date with the exception of accelerated procurements, which shall appear not less than three business days before the bid opening date
nlc: Verify that the notice of solicitation is published at least once in the City Record and is posted on the City’s website in a publicly accessible location simultaneously with its publication not less than fifteen days before the bid opening date, except for accelerated procurements, which must appear not less than three business days before the bid opening date.

rule: Such notice shall include: ((a)) agency name; ((b)) PIN; ((c)) title and/or brief description of the goods, services, or construction to be procured; ((d)) estimated quantity, if any; ((e)) how the solicitation documents may be obtained; ((f)) date and time by which, and the location where, bids must be submitted and will be publicly opened and if applicable, a link to access the livestreaming of the opening, reading, and identification of bids on a publicly accessible website; and ((g)) required vendor qualifications or eligibility requirements, if any and ((h)) A statement, if applicable, that the solicitation is subject to Section 6-129 of the Administrative Code (M/WBE and EBE program)
nlc: Verify that each notice includes the following: (a) agency name, (b) PIN, (c) title and/or brief description of the goods, services, or construction to be procured, (d) estimated quantity, if any, (e) how the solicitation documents may be obtained, (f) date and time by which, and the location where, bids must be submitted and will be publicly opened, and if applicable, a link to access the livestreaming of the opening, reading, and identification of bids on a publicly accessible website, (g) required vendor qualifications or eligibility requirements, if any, and (h) a statement, if applicable, that the solicitation is subject to Section 6-129 of the Administrative Code (M/WBE and EBE program).
dc: ["AgencyName IS NULL"], ["PIN IS NULL"], ["ShortTitle IS NULL"], ["AddressToRequest IS NULL"], ["DueDate IS NULL"], ["EventStreetAddress1 IS NULL"], ["DocumentLinks IS NULL"], ["SelectionMethodDescription IS NULL"]
sql: SELECT * FROM contract_award WHERE AgencyName IS NULL OR PIN IS NULL OR ShortTitle IS NULL OR AddressToRequest IS NULL OR DueDate IS NULL OR EventStreetAddress1 IS NULL OR DocumentLinks IS NULL OR SelectionMethodDescription IS NULL

rule: Notice of vendor selection exceeding the small purchase limits shall be published once in the City Record within fifteen days after registration of the contract
nlc: Verify that notice of vendor selection exceeding the small purchase limits is published once in the City Record within fifteen days after registration of the contract.

rule: Such notice shall include: (A) agency name; (B) PIN; (C) title and/or brief description of the goods, services, or construction procured; (D) name and address of the vendor; (E) dollar value of the contract; and (F) procurement method by which the contract was let
nlc: Verify that each notice includes the agency name, PIN, title and/or brief description of the goods, services, or construction procured, name and address of the vendor, dollar value of the contract, and procurement method by which the contract was let.
dc: ["AgencyName IS NULL"], ["PIN IS NULL"], ["ShortTitle IS NULL"], ["VendorName IS NULL"], ["VendorAddress IS NULL"], ["ContractAmount IS NULL"], ["SelectionMethodDescription IS NULL"]
sql: SELECT * FROM contract_award WHERE AgencyName IS NULL OR PIN IS NULL OR ShortTitle IS NULL OR VendorName IS NULL OR VendorAddress IS NULL OR ContractAmount IS  NULL OR SelectionMethodDescription IS NULL

rule: A copy of the IFB shall be available for public inspection at a location in the agency offices designated by the ACCO
nlc: Verify that a copy of the IFB is available for public inspection at a location in the agency offices designated by the ACCO.

rule: The CCPO shall compile citywide lists of vendors interested in being solicited for bids
nlc: Verify that the CCPO compiles citywide lists of vendors interested in being solicited for bids.

rule: the CCPO may authorize one or more agencies to maintain citywide or agency bidders lists
nlc: Check if the CCPO has authorized one or more agencies to maintain citywide or agency bidders lists.

rule: Bidders lists shall be classified by standard categories of goods, services, and construction that are sufficiently detailed to provide meaningful distinctions among categories
nlc: Verify that bidders lists are classified by standard categories of goods, services, and construction that are sufficiently detailed to provide meaningful distinctions among categories.

rule: Bidders lists shall include the names, addresses, EIN, e-mail addresses, and telephone numbers of the vendors
nlc: Verify that bidders lists include the names, addresses, EIN, e-mail addresses, and telephone numbers of the vendors.
dc: ["VendorName IS NULL"], ["VendorAddress IS NULL"], ["Email IS NULL"], ["ContactPhone IS NULL"]
sql: SELECT * FROM contract_award WHERE VendorName IS NULL OR VendorAddress IS  NULL OR Email IS NULL OR ContactPhone IS NULL

rule: bidders lists shall indicate which of the listed businesses have been certified by DSBS as minority owned, women owned and emerging business enterprises
nlc: Verify that bidders lists indicate which of the listed businesses have been certified by DSBS as minority owned, women owned, and emerging business enterprises.

rule: The CCPO and agencies, if authorized by the CCPO to maintain bidders lists, shall cause to be continuously published in the City Record notice of the availability of applications for vendors to be added to citywide bidders lists or agency bidders lists for goods, services, and construction regularly procured by the City
nlc: Verify that the CCPO and authorized agencies continuously publish in the City Record notice of the availability of applications for vendors to be added to citywide or agency bidders lists for goods, services, and construction regularly procured by the City.

rule: Every effort shall be made to publish notice in a manner that encourages minority, women and emerging business enterprises to certify with DSBS
nlc: Verify that notices are published in a manner that encourages minority, women, and emerging business enterprises to certify with DSBS.

rule: Application by vendors for placement on the citywide bidders lists or an agency’s bidders list shall be continuously available on request from the vendor
nlc: Verify that applications by vendors for placement on the citywide bidders lists or an agency’s bidders list are continuously available upon request from the vendor.

rule: Vendors that fail to respond to solicitations or notices of availability of procurement opportunities on three consecutive invitations within one standard category may be removed by the CCPO from the applicable bidders list or by the ACCO from the applicable agency bidder list after notice to the vendor
nlc: Check if any vendors have failed to respond to solicitations or notices of availability of procurement opportunities on three consecutive invitations within one standard category, and if so, ensure they have been notified and considered for removal by the CCPO from the applicable bidders list or by the ACCO from the applicable agency bidder list.

rule: A “No Bid” statement on a returned bid shall be considered a response
nlc: Verify that a 'No Bid' statement on a returned bid is considered a response.

rule: Vendors may also be removed from a citywide bidders list pursuant to procedures prescribed by the CCPO
nlc: Check if vendors have been removed from a citywide bidders list according to the procedures prescribed by the CCPO.

rule: Application for reinstatement shall be the responsibility of the vendor
nlc: Verify that the application for reinstatement is the responsibility of the vendor.

rule: Unless otherwise provided, inclusion or exclusion of the name of a vendor on a bidders list does not indicate that the vendor is responsible in respect to a particular procurement or otherwise is capable of successfully performing a City contract
nlc: Verify that the inclusion or exclusion of a vendor's name on a bidders list does not indicate the vendor's responsibility for a particular procurement or capability to successfully perform a City contract, unless otherwise provided.

rule: In accordance with these Rules, bids may be solicited from a list of prequalified vendors
nlc: Verify that bids are solicited only from a list of prequalified vendors.

rule: Pre-bid or pre-solicitation conferences may be conducted by the ACCO to explain the procurement requirements
nlc: Check if the ACCO conducts pre-bid or pre-solicitation conferences to explain the procurement requirements.

rule: Written notice of any conference shall be provided to all prospective vendors
nlc: Verify that written notice of any conference is provided to all prospective vendors.

rule: A pre-bid conference should be held long enough after the IFB has been issued to allow bidders to become familiar with the solicitation documents, but sufficiently before bid opening to allow consideration of the conference results in preparing their bids
nlc: Verify that a pre-bid conference is held long enough after the IFB has been issued to allow bidders to become familiar with the solicitation documents, and sufficiently before bid opening to allow consideration of the conference results in preparing their bids.

rule: Nothing stated at the pre-bid conference shall change the IFB unless a change is made by amendment as provided in this section
nlc: Verify that no changes to the IFB are made based on statements from the pre-bid conference unless those changes are made by amendment as provided in this section.

rule: A summary or transcript of the conference shall be prepared and shall be made available and shall be posted on the City’s website
nlc: Verify that a summary or transcript of the conference is prepared, made available, and posted on the City’s website.

rule: A record of attendance shall be kept of all conferences, and shall be made available and shall be posted on the City’s website
nlc: Verify that a record of attendance is kept for all conferences, is made available, and is posted on the City’s website.

rule: The ACCO shall authorize the issuance of any amendment
nlc: Verify that the ACCO has authorized the issuance of any amendment.

rule: Each amendment to an IFB shall be identified as such, shall be set forth in writing, and shall require that the bidder acknowledge receipt of all amendments issued as a condition for consideration of its bid
nlc: Verify that each amendment to an IFB is identified as an amendment, is set forth in writing, and requires that the bidder acknowledges receipt of all amendments issued as a condition for consideration of its bid.

rule: The amendment shall reference the portion of the IFB it amends
nlc: Verify that the amendment references the portion of the IFB it amends.

rule: Amendments shall be sent to all prospective vendors known to have received an IFB
nlc: Verify that amendments are sent to all prospective vendors known to have received an IFB.

rule: Amendments shall be distributed within a reasonable time to allow prospective vendors to consider them in preparing their bids
nlc: Verify that amendments are distributed within a reasonable time to allow prospective vendors to consider them in preparing their bids.

rule: If the time and date set for receipt of bids will not permit such preparation, such time shall be increased to the extent possible, and stated in the amendment or shall be communicated by electronic mail, facsimile, or telephone and confirmed in the amendment
nlc: Verify that if the time and date set for receipt of bids do not permit preparation, the time is increased to the extent possible and stated in the amendment, or communicated by electronic mail, facsimile, or telephone and confirmed in the amendment.

rule: A notice of amendment shall be posted on the City’s website
nlc: Verify that a notice of amendment is posted on the City’s website.

rule: Bids may be modified or withdrawn by written notice received in the office designated in the IFB before the time and date set for bid opening
nlc: Verify that any modifications or withdrawals of bids are accompanied by a written notice received in the designated office before the time and date set for bid opening.

rule: If a bid is withdrawn in accordance with this section, the bid security, if any, shall be returned to the bidder
nlc: Verify that for any bid withdrawn in accordance with this section, the bid security, if any, is returned to the bidder.

rule: Any bid received at the location designated in the solicitation after the time and date set for receipt of bids is late and shall not be considered
nlc: Verify that no bid received at the location designated in the solicitation after the time and date set for receipt of bids is considered.

rule: Any request for withdrawal or modification received at the location designated in the solicitation after the time and date set for receipt of bids is late and shall not be considered
nlc: Verify that any request for withdrawal or modification received at the designated location after the specified time and date for receipt of bids is not considered.

rule: Late bids and modifications shall not be opened until after registration of the contract
nlc: Verify that late bids and modifications are not opened until after the registration of the contract.

rule: A late modification of a successful bid that makes its terms more favorable to the City shall be considered at any time it is received and may be accepted upon the approval of the ACCO
nlc: Verify that any late modification of a successful bid that makes its terms more favorable to the City is considered at any time it is received and is accepted only upon the approval of the ACCO.

rule: Upon its receipt, each bid and modification shall be time- and date-stamped, but not opened, and stored in a secure location until the time and date set for bid opening
nlc: Verify that upon receipt, each bid and modification is time- and date-stamped, is not opened, and is stored in a secure location until the time and date set for bid opening.

rule: Before bid opening the agency may not disclose the identity of any bidder
nlc: Verify that the agency does not disclose the identity of any bidder before bid opening.

rule: Bids and modifications will be opened publicly, at the time, date, and location designated in the IFB
nlc: Verify that bids and modifications are opened publicly at the time, date, and location designated in the IFB.

rule: The name of each bidder, the bid price, and such other information as is deemed appropriate will be read aloud or otherwise made available
nlc: Verify that the name of each bidder, the bid price, and any other deemed appropriate information are read aloud or otherwise made available.

rule: These requirements may be met through access to a computer terminal at the location where bids are to be opened, provided that paper documents are available upon request at the time of in-person bid opening
nlc: Verify that access to a computer terminal is available at the location where bids are to be opened, and ensure that paper documents are available upon request at the time of in-person bid opening.

rule: This information also will be recorded at the time of bid opening
nlc: Verify that this information is recorded at the time of bid opening.

rule: The bids will be tabulated or a bid abstract prepared and made available for public inspection
nlc: Verify that the bids are either tabulated or a bid abstract is prepared and made available for public inspection.

rule: The opened bids will be available for public inspection within five days of bid opening but in any case before vendor selection except to the extent the bidder designates trade secrets or other proprietary data to be confidential
nlc: Verify that all opened bids are available for public inspection within five days of bid opening and before vendor selection, except for parts designated as trade secrets or proprietary data by the bidder.

rule: Material so designated must accompany the bid and must be readily separable from the bid in order to facilitate public inspection of the nonconfidential portion of the bid
nlc: Verify that the designated material accompanies the bid and is readily separable from the bid to facilitate public inspection of the nonconfidential portion of the bid.

rule: Prices, makes, and model or catalog numbers of the items offered, deliveries, and terms of payment will be publicly available at a reasonable time after bid opening but in any event before vendor selection regardless of any designation to the contrary at the time of bid opening
nlc: Verify that prices, makes, model or catalog numbers of the items offered, deliveries, and terms of payment are publicly available at a reasonable time after bid opening and before vendor selection, regardless of any designation to the contrary at the time of bid opening.

rule: For bids on construction contracts submitted in accordance with Section 3-02(b)(2)(xx)(J) of these Rules, the sealed list of subcontractors submitted with the low bid will be opened after such low bid has been announced and the names of the subcontractors shall be announced
nlc: Verify that for bids on construction contracts submitted in accordance with Section 3-02(b)(2)(xx)(J), the sealed list of subcontractors submitted with the low bid is opened after the low bid has been announced and the names of the subcontractors are announced.

rule: The sealed lists of subcontractors submitted by all other bidders pursuant to Section 3-02(b)(2)(xx)(J) of these Rules will be returned to such bidders unopened after the contract award
nlc: Verify that the sealed lists of subcontractors submitted by all other bidders are returned to them unopened after the contract award.

rule: The ACCO shall examine the bids to determine the validity of any requests for nondisclosure of trade secrets and other proprietary data identified in writing
nlc: Verify that the ACCO examines the bids to determine the validity of any requests for nondisclosure of trade secrets and other proprietary data identified in writing.

rule: Nondisclosure is permissible only if approved by Agency Counsel
nlc: Verify that nondisclosure is approved by Agency Counsel before it is considered permissible.

rule: Any decision not to honor a request for confidentiality shall be communicated in writing to the bidder making the submission
nlc: Verify that any decision not to honor a request for confidentiality is communicated in writing to the bidder making the submission.

rule: Correction or withdrawal of a bid because of an inadvertent, nonjudgmental mistake in the bid requires careful consideration to protect the integrity of the competitive bidding system, and to assure fairness
nlc: Verify that any correction or withdrawal of a bid due to an inadvertent, nonjudgmental mistake is carefully considered to protect the integrity of the competitive bidding system and assure fairness.

rule: If the mistake is attributable to an error in judgment, the bid may not be corrected
nlc: Check if any bid that is attributable to an error in judgment has not been corrected.

rule: Bid correction or withdrawal by reason of a non-judgmental mistake is permissible, but only to the extent that it is not contrary to the interest of the City or the fair treatment of other bidders
nlc: Verify that any bid correction or withdrawal due to a non-judgmental mistake is not contrary to the interest of the City and does not affect the fair treatment of other bidders.

rule: A bidder may correct mistakes discovered before the time and date set for bid opening by withdrawing or correcting the bid as provided in Section 3-02(j) of these Rules
nlc: Check if any bidder has corrected mistakes by withdrawing or correcting the bid before the time and date set for bid opening, as provided in Section 3-02(j) of these Rules.

rule: When the Contracting Officer knows or has reason to conclude after bids have been publicly opened that a mistake has been made, such officer shall request from the bidder written verification of the bid
nlc: Verify that the Contracting Officer requests written verification of the bid from the bidder when they know or have reason to conclude after bids have been publicly opened that a mistake has been made.

rule: If the bidder alleges mistake, the bid may be corrected or withdrawn upon approval of the ACCO if the following conditions are met
nlc: Verify that if a bidder alleges a mistake, the bid is corrected or withdrawn only upon approval of the ACCO and the specified conditions are met.

rule: Minor informalities are matters of form, rather than substance, evident from the bid document or insignificant mistakes that can be waived or corrected without prejudice to other bidders; that is, the effect on price, quantity, quality, delivery, or contractual conditions is negligible
nlc: Verify that any minor informalities in the bid document are matters of form, not substance, and that they are insignificant mistakes that can be waived or corrected without affecting price, quantity, quality, delivery, or contractual conditions.

rule: The Contracting Officer may waive such informalities or allow the bidder to correct them depending on which is in the best interest of the City
nlc: Check if the Contracting Officer has waived informalities or allowed the bidder to correct them based on the best interest of the City.

rule: If the mistake and the intended correct bid are clearly evident on the face of the bid document, the bid shall be corrected to the intended correct bid and may not be withdrawn
nlc: Verify that if the mistake and the intended correct bid are clearly evident on the face of the bid document, the bid is corrected to the intended correct bid and is not withdrawn.

rule: Mistakes may not be corrected after bid opening
nlc: Verify that no mistakes are corrected after bid opening.

rule: A bidder may be permitted to withdraw a bid where a unilateral error or mistake has been discovered in the bid and the Contracting Officer makes the following determination, which shall be approved by the ACCO
nlc: Verify that a bidder is permitted to withdraw a bid only if a unilateral error or mistake has been discovered in the bid and the Contracting Officer has made the determination, which is approved by the ACCO.

rule: Upon the approval of the ACCO, the bid may be withdrawn, and the bid bond or other security returned to the bidder
nlc: Verify that once the ACCO has approved, the bid is withdrawn and the bid bond or other security is returned to the bidder.

rule: If the bid was the low bid or the bid that represents best value, then the contract shall either be awarded to the next lowest bidder or bidder that represents the next best value to the City, as appropriate, or resolicited pursuant to these Rules
nlc: Verify that if a bid was the low bid or represents the best value, the contract is either awarded to the next lowest bidder or the bidder that represents the next best value to the City, or the contract is resolicited according to these Rules.

rule: Under no circumstances shall a bid be amended or revised to rectify the error or mistake
nlc: Verify that no bid is amended or revised to rectify any error or mistake.

rule: Mistakes shall not be corrected after vendor selection except where the ACCO, subject to the approval of the CCPO, makes a determination that it would be unconscionable not to allow the mistake to be corrected
nlc: Verify that no mistakes are corrected after vendor selection unless the ACCO, with the approval of the CCPO, has determined that it would be unconscionable not to allow the correction.

rule: When a bid is corrected or withdrawn, or correction or withdrawal is denied, the ACCO shall prepare a determination showing that the relief was granted or denied in accordance with these Rules
nlc: Verify that when a bid is corrected or withdrawn, or when correction or withdrawal is denied, the ACCO has prepared a determination showing that the relief was granted or denied in accordance with these Rules.

rule: Except as provided for in Section 3-02(j) of these Rules a bidder may not withdraw its bid before the expiration of forty-five days after the date of the opening of bids; thereafter, a bidder may withdraw its bid only in writing and in advance of an actual award
nlc: Verify that no bidder withdraws its bid before the expiration of forty-five days after the date of the opening of bids, except as provided for in Section 3-02(j) of these Rules. Additionally, ensure that any bid withdrawal after this period is done in writing and in advance of an actual award.

rule: The responsible bidder whose bid meets the requirements and objectively measurable evaluation criteria set forth in the IFB, and whose bid price is the lowest responsive and responsible bid price or, if the IFB has so stated, the lowest responsive and responsible evaluated bid price, shall be selected for the contract
nlc: Verify that the selected bidder is the one whose bid meets the requirements and objectively measurable evaluation criteria set forth in the IFB, and whose bid price is the lowest responsive and responsible bid price or, if the IFB has so stated, the lowest responsive and responsible evaluated bid price.

rule: A bid shall not be evaluated for any requirement or criterion that is not disclosed in the IFB
nlc: Verify that no bid is evaluated for any requirement or criterion that is not disclosed in the IFB.

rule: Prior to the bid, the ACCO will determine whether the goods or standard services will be awarded to the lowest responsive and responsible bidder or to the responsive and responsible bidder whose bid represents the best value to the City
nlc: Verify that prior to the bid, the ACCO determines if the goods or standard services are awarded to either the lowest responsive and responsible bidder or the responsive and responsible bidder whose bid represents the best value to the City.

rule: The responsive and responsible bidder whose bid meets the requirements and objectively measurable evaluation criteria set forth in the IFB, and whose bid price is the lowest, or whose bid represents the best value to the City by optimizing quality, cost and efficiency, will be selected for the contract
nlc: Verify that the selected bidder is responsive and responsible, meets the requirements and objectively measurable evaluation criteria set forth in the IFB, and either has the lowest bid price or represents the best value to the City by optimizing quality, cost, and efficiency.

rule: If award will be made based on best value, best value may be determined by the ACCO, or the ACCO may convene a committee to make such determination
nlc: Verify that if an award is made based on best value, the best value is determined by the ACCO or a committee convened by the ACCO.

rule: Any such committee must consist of persons with knowledge, expertise and experience sufficient to make a fair and reasonable determination
nlc: Verify that any such committee consists of persons with knowledge, expertise, and experience sufficient to make a fair and reasonable determination.

rule: As set forth below the ACCO, or the committee as the case may be, may determine best value by consideration of price together with other factors deemed relevant by the ACCO and set forth in the IFB
nlc: Verify that the ACCO or the committee determines best value by considering price along with other factors deemed relevant by the ACCO and set forth in the IFB.

rule: In making such determination the ACCO, or committee, must consider the low responsive bid and the next low responsive bids that are within ten percent (10%) of the low responsive bid in price, including any bids that have been adjusted pursuant to subparagraph (iv) of this paragraph, or such higher percentage as approved by the CCPO either on an individual basis or by category or class
nlc: Verify that the ACCO or committee considers the low responsive bid and the next low responsive bids that are within ten percent of the low responsive bid in price, including any bids adjusted pursuant to subparagraph (iv), or such higher percentage as approved by the CCPO on an individual basis or by category or class.

rule: The ACCO, or committee, may consider any and all information related to such factors in determining best value and may require additional information to be submitted by the bidders with the bid, or alternatively, within up to thirty (30) days from the bid opening from all bidders whose bids are to be considered pursuant to in Section 3-02(o)(1)(iii) of these Rules
nlc: Verify that the ACCO or committee has considered all relevant information related to determining best value and has required additional information to be submitted by the bidders either with the bid or within thirty (30) days from the bid opening for all bidders whose bids are to be considered according to Section 3-02(o)(1)(iii) of these Rules.

rule: If a committee is used to evaluate the bids, then written evaluation forms shall be completed to record the evaluation of the bids and shall be signed and dated by all members of the committee
nlc: Verify that if a committee is used to evaluate the bids, written evaluation forms are completed to record the evaluation of the bids and are signed and dated by all members of the committee.

rule: If award will be made based on best value, a vendor that is an M/WBE or State-certified M/WBE must, except with the approval of the CCPO, be given a price preference of 10% and will be evaluated as if the bid price were 10% lower
nlc: Verify that if an award is made based on best value, a vendor that is an M/WBE or State-certified M/WBE is given a price preference of 10% and is evaluated as if the bid price were 10% lower, unless approved otherwise by the CCPO.

rule: A price preference of a different percentage may be given with approval from the CCPO
nlc: Check if any price preference of a different percentage has been given with approval from the CCPO.

rule: The price preference percentage, if any, shall be included in the IFB
nlc: Verify that the price preference percentage, if any, is included in the IFB.

rule: Upon determination of the apparent lowest responsive and responsible bidder or responsive and responsible bidder providing best value, pursuant to Section 3-02(0)(1) of these Rules, and prior to award, the Contracting Officer may elect to open negotiations with the selected vendor in an effort to improve the bid to the City with respect to the price only if award will be made to the lowest responsive and responsible bidder, or if award will be made to the responsive and responsible bidder whose bid represents the best value to the City, with respect to any of the factors considered in determining best value
nlc: Verify that negotiations are opened with the selected vendor to improve the bid price only if the award is made to the lowest responsive and responsible bidder or to the bidder whose bid represents the best value to the City, considering the factors for determining best value.

rule: In the event the apparent winning bidder declines to negotiate, the Contracting Officer may elect to either award the contract to the apparent winning bidder or may, upon written approval by the ACCO, reject all bids in accordance with this section
nlc: Check if, when the apparent winning bidder declines to negotiate, the Contracting Officer either awards the contract to the apparent winning bidder or, with written approval by the ACCO, rejects all bids.

rule: The result of negotiations, if any, shall be documented in the Recommendation for Award
nlc: Verify that the result of any negotiations is documented in the Recommendation for Award.

rule: Upon the determination of the apparent winning bidder pursuant to Section 3-02(o)(1) of these Rules, a Recommendation for Award shall be approved by the ACCO and the contract shall be awarded to that bidder
nlc: Verify that upon the determination of the apparent winning bidder pursuant to Section 3-02(o)(1), a Recommendation for Award is approved by the ACCO and the contract is awarded to that bidder.

rule: Where the award is based on best value to the City, the ACCO shall set forth in the Recommendation for Award the reasons that the bid represents the best value to the City and the factors considered by the agency
nlc: Verify that when the award is based on best value to the City, the ACCO has set forth in the Recommendation for Award the reasons that the bid represents the best value to the City and the factors considered by the agency.

rule: In the case of low tie bids, the ACCO shall break the tie in the following order of priority. (i) Select a certified New York City minority owned, woman-owned or emerging business entity bidder; (ii) Select a New York City bidder; (iii) Select a certified New York State small, minority or woman-owned business bidder; (iv) Select a New York State bidder; (v) Conduct a drawing. Tie bidders shall be invited to witness the drawing
nlc: Verify that in the case of low tie bids, the ACCO breaks the tie by first selecting a certified New York City minority owned, woman-owned, or emerging business entity bidder. If none, select a New York City bidder. If still tied, select a certified New York State small, minority, or woman-owned business bidder. If still tied, select a New York State bidder. If still tied, conduct a drawing and invite tie bidders to witness the drawing.

rule: A witness shall be present to verify the drawing and shall certify the results on the bid tabulation sheet
nlc: Verify that a witness is present to verify the drawing and has certified the results on the bid tabulation sheet.

rule: A record shall be made of tie bids received, and the method used to break the tie
nlc: Verify that a record is made of tie bids received and the method used to break the tie.

rule: The ACCO shall monitor the incidence of low tie bids in a systematic manner
nlc: Verify that the ACCO is systematically monitoring the incidence of low tie bids.

rule: When a single bid has been received in response to an IFB, a vendor may be selected only after the ACCO has determined that a sufficient number of other potentially responsive vendors have had a reasonable opportunity to bid; why, as a result of inquiries made by the agency, other vendors chose not to submit bids; that the bid submitted meets minimum requirements of the IFB; that the price is fair and reasonable, and that resolicitation is not in the best interest of the City
nlc: Verify that when a single bid is received in response to an IFB, a vendor is selected only after the ACCO has determined that a sufficient number of other potentially responsive vendors had a reasonable opportunity to bid, the reasons why other vendors chose not to submit bids as a result of agency inquiries, the bid submitted meets the minimum requirements of the IFB, the price is fair and reasonable, and resolicitation is not in the best interest of the City.

rule: Unless alternate bids are requested in the solicitation, such bids may not be accepted
nlc: Verify that alternate bids are not accepted unless they are requested in the solicitation.

rule: If the ACCO determines that a bidder is either not responsible or not responsive, such bidder shall immediately be notified in writing of such determination and the reasons therefor, and the right to appeal the determination, if applicable
nlc: Verify that any bidder determined by the ACCO to be either not responsible or not responsive is immediately notified in writing of the determination, the reasons for the determination, and the right to appeal the determination, if applicable.

rule: A copy of the notification shall be filed with the CCPO and Comptroller
nlc: Verify that a copy of the notification is filed with both the CCPO and Comptroller.

rule: Multiple award task order contracts for standard services or multiple award purchase order contracts for goods may be awarded upon a determination by the ACCO that it is in the best interest of the City to award multiple contracts for goods or standard services to multiple contractors and to allocate work among such contractors through a task order or purchase order system
nlc: Verify that multiple award task order contracts for standard services or multiple award purchase order contracts for goods are awarded only upon a determination by the ACCO that it is in the best interest of the City to award multiple contracts for goods or standard services to multiple contractors and to allocate work among such contractors through a task order or purchase order system.

rule: The criteria to be considered by the ACCO in making such determination may include the following: the nature of the goods or standard services to be procured; the expected frequency of task order or purchase order issuance; the capacity of vendors to provide all of the required services within the required timeframes; and the potential advantage of multiple contracts (e.g., more favorable terms; more competitive pricing, etc.)
nlc: Verify that the ACCO considers the following criteria in making determinations: the nature of the goods or standard services to be procured, the expected frequency of task order or purchase order issuance, the capacity of vendors to provide all of the required services within the required timeframes, and the potential advantage of multiple contracts, such as more favorable terms and more competitive pricing.

rule: Multiple awards may be made for contracts for goods or standard services, pursuant to competitive sealed bids where award is made based on price only, or based on best value pursuant to the criteria set forth in Section 3-02(o)(1) of these Rules, in conjunction with the procedures prescribed in this subdivision.
nlc: Verify that multiple awards for contracts for goods or standard services are made pursuant to competitive sealed bids, either based on price only or based on best value according to the criteria in Section 3-02(o)(1), following the prescribed procedures.

rule: The IFB shall also state the procedures and criteria to be used in selecting the vendor to perform on an individual task order or goods to be purchased pursuant to an individual purchase order
nlc: Verify that the IFB states the procedures and criteria used in selecting the vendor for an individual task order or goods to be purchased pursuant to an individual purchase order.

rule: The agency may: (A) select the vendor that represents the best value to the City for that particular task order or purchase order, as determined pursuant to Section 3-02(o) of these Rules, based on each vendor’s bid
nlc: Verify that the agency selects the vendor that represents the best value to the City for each task order or purchase order, as determined pursuant to Section 3-02(o) of these Rules, based on each vendor’s bid.

rule: the agency may solicit offers for each task order or purchase order from all awarded vendors
nlc: Check if the agency solicits offers for each task order or purchase order from all awarded vendors.

rule: If the agency solicits offers for each task order or purchase order, each vendor shall receive each solicitation and have a reasonable opportunity to compete to provide the standard services or goods
nlc: Verify that for each task order or purchase order where the agency solicits offers, each vendor receives the solicitation and has a reasonable opportunity to compete to provide the standard services or goods.

rule: The agency may set forth an alternative method of assigning task orders or purchase orders if it is determined by the CCPO to be in the City’s best interest and is set forth in the IFB
nlc: Check if the agency has set forth an alternative method of assigning task orders or purchase orders that is determined by the CCPO to be in the City’s best interest and is set forth in the IFB.

rule: In the event that such alternative method is used for standard services, each vendor with a contract shall receive notice of assignment of each task order at the time each task order is issued, regardless of whether each vendor with a contract received the solicitation for the task order
nlc: Verify that each vendor with a contract receives notice of assignment of each task order at the time each task order is issued when an alternative method is used for standard services, regardless of whether the vendor received the solicitation for the task order.

rule: rotation, or other non-discretionary method of assignment, including where assignment pursuant to such method may be varied based on stated criteria
nlc: Verify that assignments are made using rotation or another non-discretionary method, and check if variations in assignments are based on stated criteria.

rule: In the event that a vendor selected pursuant to one of the selection methods in paragraphs (i) or (ii) above is unable to perform the services on an individual task order or provide the goods to be purchased pursuant to an individual purchase order for reasons such as lack of capacity or conflict of interest, the agency may disqualify that vendor for purposes of that task order and select another vendor with approval of the CCPO
nlc: Check if any vendor selected through the specified selection methods is unable to perform services or provide goods due to lack of capacity or conflict of interest, and verify that the agency disqualifies such a vendor and selects another vendor with CCPO approval for that task order.

rule: Price shall be the primary factor considered in making individual vendor selection decisions, and no task order shall be issued unless the ACCO determines that the proposed price is fair and reasonable
nlc: Verify that price is the primary factor considered in making individual vendor selection decisions, and ensure that no task order is issued unless the ACCO has determined that the proposed price is fair and reasonable.

rule: Prices set forth in a multiple award contract shall represent maximum prices that may be set forth in individual task orders issued to that vendor
nlc: Verify that prices set forth in a multiple award contract represent maximum prices that are set forth in individual task orders issued to the vendor.

rule: Unless otherwise approved by the CCPO, contracts awarded pursuant to this subdivision shall have a total term including all renewals, of not more than five years, provided that any such contract may be extended for an additional period of one year beyond the date of expiration of such contract in accordance with § 4-02(b)(1)(iii) of these Rules
nlc: Verify that contracts awarded pursuant to this subdivision have a total term, including all renewals, of not more than five years unless approved by the CCPO, and check if any such contract is extended for an additional period of one year beyond the expiration date in accordance with § 4-02(b)(1)(iii) of these Rules.

rule: Task orders, or purchase orders may extend beyond the expiration of the contract term, in which event the terms and conditions of the contract shall continue to apply to the task order or purchase order until its termination or expiration
nlc: Verify that for any task order or purchase order extending beyond the expiration of the contract term, the terms and conditions of the contract continue to apply until the task order or purchase order's termination or expiration.

rule: Task orders, or purchase orders, shall have a maximum term of three years or, if issued for a specific project, until the specific project is completed
nlc: Verify that task orders or purchase orders have a maximum term of three years, or if issued for a specific project, that they are valid until the specific project is completed.

rule: Notwithstanding the above, a task order may be extended beyond, or further extended beyond, the expiration of the contract term or beyond the expiration of the task order with the CCPO’s approval, which must be obtained in a manner prescribed by the CCPO
nlc: Verify that any extension of a task order beyond the expiration of the contract term or beyond the expiration of the task order has been approved by the CCPO in the manner prescribed by the CCPO.

rule: The ACCO may reject all bids and may elect to resolicit by bid in accordance with this section or by other method authorized by these Rules
nlc: Check if the ACCO has rejected all bids and has elected to resolicit by bid in accordance with this section or by another method authorized by these Rules.

rule: Under the circumstances set forth in this subdivision, the ACCO may determine that it is appropriate to reject all bids after opening and before vendor selection and to complete the acquisition by a new solicitation for which notice is provided as set forth in this subdivision
nlc: Verify that under the specified circumstances, the ACCO has determined it is appropriate to reject all bids after opening and before vendor selection, and has completed the acquisition by a new solicitation with notice provided as specified.

rule: This procedure may be used where at least five responses to the solicitation have been received, and despite efforts to address problems in the solicitation by means of prebid conferences and issuance of amended specifications in accordance with these Rules, all responses are found to be non-responsive as a result of the same defect(s) in the specification or other solicitation documents
nlc: Verify that this procedure is used only when at least five responses to the solicitation have been received, and all responses are found to be non-responsive due to the same defect(s) in the specification or other solicitation documents, despite efforts to address these problems through prebid conferences and issuance of amended specifications.

rule: When using this procedure, the ACCO shall: (i) make a determination that: (A) five or more bids have been received in response to the original solicitation; (B) all of the bids have been found to be non-responsive as a result of the same previously unresolved defect(s) in the solicitation documents; and (C) prior to opening the bids the agency had made diligent efforts to discover and correct defects in the solicitation documents by inviting questions from prospective bidders, holding pre-bid conferences, and issuing amendments to the solicitation documents as appropriate;
nlc: Verify that when using this procedure, the ACCO has made a determination that: (A) five or more bids have been received in response to the original solicitation; (B) all of the bids have been found to be non-responsive due to the same previously unresolved defect(s) in the solicitation documents; and (C) prior to opening the bids, the agency made diligent efforts to discover and correct defects in the solicitation documents by inviting questions from prospective bidders, holding pre-bid conferences, and issuing amendments to the solicitation documents as appropriate.

rule: All bids shall be retained
nlc: Verify that all bids are retained.

rule: When bids are rejected or a solicitation canceled after bids are received, the bids shall be retained and the bid security, if any, shall be promptly returned, and the file so documented
nlc: Verify that when bids are rejected or a solicitation is canceled after bids are received, the bids are retained, the bid security, if any, is promptly returned, and the file is documented accordingly.
